items.t: GPDO amended — new Class CA and Class M change
items.b: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development etc.) (England) (Amendment) Order 2026 (SI 2026/313), in force 9 April 2026, adds Class CA (reverse vending machines in shop walls and curtilage, subject to size and siting limits), restricts rooftop structure heights under Class M, and updates references to the NPPF throughout the Order.

Consequential Amendments Regulations in force; commencement Nos 3 and 4 stage in the environmental delivery plans, nature restoration levy, CPO reforms and NSIP changes through 2026. Verify each provision before relying on it.

The Town and Country Planning (Biodiversity Net Gain) (Amendment) Regulations 2026 were laid on 13 July 2026 and come into force on 6 August 2026 in England. They add a 0.2ha small-sites exemption and a temporary-development exemption, remove the self-build/custom-build exemption, and let minor development go straight to off-site gain. Changes apply to applications made on or after 6 August 2026; verify before relying.
Authorities whose adopted housing requirement is 80% or less of local housing need must add a 20% buffer (NPPF December 2024 transitional arrangements).
A fully restructured draft was consulted December 2025 – March 2026. The December 2024 Framework remains in force until the revised version is published — watch this space.
